Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Intermediate The threat actors exploited human curiosity and trust via spear-phishing to gain initial access to the network, then used PowerShell to execute commands and establish persistence.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shared DB or directory, etc.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2010-0738 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Office Word Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The attackers sent tailored emails to specific employees with malicious attachments exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Office Word.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.kev: CVE-2012-0507 [CWE-476]: Windows Help Center Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The malicious attachment contained PowerShell scripts which were executed upon opening, allowing the attackers to run commands and download additional payloads.

Scenario.summary: The C0033 campaign targeted transportation systems using spear-phishing to gain initial access, followed by PowerShell execution and further stages to cause disruption and chaos.
